codeReview.summary: The build-type gate cleanly hides the scaffold summary only in release builds while preserving the full five-destination app shell and keeping the debug summary visible as a positive control. I did not find any high-confidence correctness issues in the implementation for the reviewed feature.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The android-feature-worker procedure still mandates writing failing tests first even for release-shell gating work where the mission guidance explicitly says release proof may need assemble/sign/install manual verification because no release instrumentation harness exists. That mismatch caused an avoidable procedure deviation for this feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/android-feature-worker/SKILL.md:22 requires "Write failing tests first (red)"; /root/.factory/missions/6abb6051-d9e4-4111-861b-5a664893e746/AGENTS.md:50 notes release-shell proof may require manually assembling/installing the release artifact; the handoff skillFeedback for worker session e51962b8-3ed4-4a00-825f-84f39dbf0305 records this exact deviation.

summary: Pass. The commit gates `ShellSummaryRow` behind a release-only `BuildConfig.SHOW_SHELL_SUMMARY` flag, preserves the normal `aterm` shell and all five top-level destinations, strengthens the debug instrumentation positive control, and the recorded release validation covers launch, navigation, and relaunch without scaffold leakage.
